pokemon:ariados.name: Ariados
pokemon:ariados.summary: Ariados’s feet are tipped with tiny hooked claws that enable it to scuttle on ceilings and vertical walls. This Pokémon constricts the foe with thin and strong silk webbing.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Ariados’s feet are tipped with tiny hooked claws that enable it to scuttle on ceilings and vertical walls. This Pokémon constricts the foe with thin and strong silk webbing.
It attaches silk to its prey and sets it free. Later, it tracks the silk to the prey and its friends.
It attaches silk to its prey and sets it free. Later, it tracks the silk to the prey and its friends.
Rather than mak­ ing a nest in one specific spot, it wanders in search of food after darkness falls.
It attaches silk to its prey and sets it free. Later, it tracks the silk to the prey and its friends.
Its feet are tipped with tiny hooked claws that enable it to scuttle on ceilings and vertical walls. It constricts its foe with thin and strong silk webbing.
A single strand of a special string is endlessly spun out of its rear. The string leads back to its nest.
It spins string not only from its rear but also from its mouth. It is hard to tell which end is which.
It spins string not only from its rear but also from its mouth. It’s hard to tell which end is which.
It spins string not only from its rear but also from its mouth. It is hard to tell which end is which.
It spins thread from both its rear and its mouth. Then it wraps its prey up in thread and sips their bodily fluids at its leisure.
Ariados’s feet are tipped with tiny hooked claws that enable it to scuttle on ceilings and vertical walls. This Pokémon constricts the foe with thin and strong silk webbing.
It attaches silk to its prey and sets it free. Later, it tracks the silk to the prey and its friends.
It attaches silk to its prey and sets it free. Later, it tracks the silk to the prey and its friends.
ARIADOS’s feet are tipped with tiny hooked claws that enable it to scuttle on ceilings and vertical walls. This POKéMON constricts the foe with thin and strong silk webbing.
ARIADOS’s feet are tipped with tiny hooked claws that enable it to scuttle on ceilings and vertical walls. This POKéMON constricts the foe with thin and strong silk webbing.
A single strand of a special string is endlessly spun out of its rear. The string leads back to its nest.
A single strand of a special string is endlessly spun out of its rear. The string leads back to its nest.
There are some areas where people use the string Ariados spins for their own weaving. The resulting cloth is popular for its strength.
It spews threads from its mouth to catch its prey. When night falls, it leaves its web to go hunt aggressively.
Every night, it wanders around in search of prey, whose movements it restrains by spewing threads before it bites into them with its fangs.
It attaches silk to its prey and sets it free. Later, it tracks the silk to the prey and its friends.
It attaches silk to its prey and sets it free. Later, it tracks the silk to the prey and its friends.
It spins string not only from its rear but also from its mouth. It’s hard to tell which end is which.
It attaches silk to its prey and sets it free. Later, it tracks the silk to the prey and its friends.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Ariados’s feet are tipped with tiny hooked claws that enable it to scuttle on ceilings and vertical walls. This Pokémon constricts the foe with thin and strong silk webbing.

pokemon:spinarak.name: Spinarak
pokemon:spinarak.summary: The web spun by Spinarak can be considered its second nervous system. It is said that this Pokémon can determine what kind of prey is touching its web just by the tiny vibrations it feels through the web’s strands.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: The web spun by Spinarak can be considered its second nervous system. It is said that this Pokémon can determine what kind of prey is touching its web just by the tiny vibrations it feels through the web’s strands.
It sets a trap by spinning a web with thin but strong silk. It waits motionlessly for prey to arrive.
It sets a trap by spinning a web with thin but strong silk. It waits motionlessly for prey to arrive.
If prey becomes ensnared in its nest of spun string, it waits motionlessly until it becomes dark.
It sets a trap by spinning a web with thin but strong silk. It waits motionlessly for prey to arrive.
The web it spins can be considered its second nervous system. It is said that a SPINARAK determines its prey by the tiny vibrations it feels through the web.
It spins a web using fine--but durable-- thread. It then waits patiently for prey to be trapped.
It lies still in the same pose for days in its web, waiting for its unsuspecting prey to wander close.
It lies still in the same pose for days in its web, waiting for its unsuspecting prey to wander close.
It lies still in the same pose for days in its web, waiting for its unsuspecting prey to wander close.
Some fishermen weave its sturdy thread into nets to catch fish Pokémon.

The web spun by Spinarak can be considered its second nervous system. It is said that this Pokémon can determine what kind of prey is touching its web just by the tiny vibrations it feels through the web’s strands.
It sets a trap by spinning a web with thin but strong silk. It waits motionlessly for prey to arrive.
It sets a trap by spinning a web with thin but strong silk. It waits motionlessly for prey to arrive.

The web spun by SPINARAK can be considered its second nervous system. It is said that this POKéMON can determine what kind of prey is touching its web just by the tiny vibrations it feels through the web’s strands.

The web spun by SPINARAK can be considered its second nervous system. It is said that this POKéMON can determine what kind of prey is touching its web just by the tiny vibrations it feels through the web’s strands.
It spins a web using fine--but durable--thread. It then waits pa­ tiently for prey to be trapped.
It spins a web using fine--but durable--thread. It then waits patiently for prey to be trapped.
It waits intently until its preferred prey, Cutiefly, gets caught in its web. In fact, it’s quite a patient Pokémon.
Although the poison from its fangs isn’t that strong, it’s potent enough to weaken prey that gets caught in its web.
With threads from its mouth, it fashions sturdy webs that won’t break even if you set a rock on them.
It sets a trap by spinning a web with thin but strong silk. It waits motionlessly for prey to arrive.
It sets a trap by spinning a web with thin but strong silk. It waits motionlessly for prey to arrive.
It spins a web using fine--but durable--thread. It then waits patiently for prey to be trapped.
It lies still in the same pose for days in its web, waiting for its unsuspecting prey to wander close.

loreSummary: The web spun by Spinarak can be considered its second nervous system. It is said that this Pokémon can determine what kind of prey is touching its web just by the tiny vibrations it feels through the web’s strands.
